Kur korumalı mevduat sistemi bugün -ne zaman çözülecek göreceğiz ama- ciddi bir yüktür. Kamu-özel iş birliği gelecek kuşakları, çocuklarımızı, torunlarımızı, doğmamış kesimlerimizi borç altına sokacak bir yapıdır ve bunun için planlama-piyasa ilişkisinin yeniden kurulması, planlama ve piyasanın kaynak tahsisini birlikte yapması gerekiyor. Tabii ki bütün bunların hepsinin, en başta da söylediğim gibi, demokrasi içinde, hukuk devleti içinde temel hak ve hürriyetlerin geliştirilerek yapılması gerekiyor.
Son olarak bütçe hakkı üzerinde durmak isterim. Bakın, bütçe sürecine ilişkin teknik birkaç şey söylemek isterim.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
BAŞKAN - Rahmi Bey, buyurun.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (Devamla) - Teşekkür ederim Sayın Başkan.
Bütçe süreci etkinlikten uzak, Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisinde bütçenin nitelikli ve kapsayıcı bir müzakeresine ihtiyaç var ve bu anlamda da Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisinin bu konuda bir ihtisasa kavuşturulması gerekiyor. Öncelikle bütçe sistematiği iyileştirilmeli, geçmişte bütçe dışı fonlar, döner sermayeler vardı -döner sermayeler devam ediyor- özel hesap, sonra ajanslar kuruluyor, vakıflar kuruluyor, bakın, olması gereken şeffaf bir bütçe, tek bir hazine, bütçenin birlik ilkesi ve hesap verilebilirliktir. Türkiye Varlık Fonu var, kur korumalı
mevduat sistemi var. Bunların hepsinin bir bütün içinde alınması gerekiyor.
2) Kalkınma planları Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisi kararı olarak gerçekleşiyor, ortaya konuyor. Biz burada... Yani bir yasalaşma süreci değil TBMM kararıdır. O zaman, kalkınma planının uygulama sonuçlarının yıllık olarak Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisine sunulmasına ihtiyaç var. Bakarız ne yapılmış ne yapılmamış, bunu görürüz. Kesin hesap komisyonunun kurulması çok büyük önem arz ediyor.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
BAŞKAN - Buyurun.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (Devamla) - Teşekkür ederim.
Kesin hesap komisyonu kurulmalı, sürekli bir komisyon, Plan ve Bütçe Komisyonuyla birlikte çalışacak, başkanı ana muhalefet partisinden olacak ve bütçe uygulama sonuçlarını her anlamda; usul, esas açısından ciddi biçimde inceleyecek. Türkiye'nin buna ihtiyacı var. (CHP sıralarından alkışlar) Türkiye'nin kaynaklarını iyi kullanmaya ihtiyacı var. Bunu Türkiye için, atalarımız için, çocuklarımız, torunlarımız için yapmak zorundayız.
Sayıştay denetimi kapsamı ve etkinliği artırılmalı. Bakın, Sayıştay Kanunu'nun 45'inci maddesi çok ilginçtir; Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisine, Sayıştaya, denetim talebinde bulunduğu herhangi bir kurumu istediği şekilde denetletebilme yetkisi verir.
Sayın Başkan, bu çok önemli bir konu; kullanılıyor mu bilmiyorum ama buna ilişkin bir soru önergesiyle de gündeme getireceğim. Yani bu konuda Türkiye Büyük Millet Meclisinin, yasamanın güçlendirilmesi, Parlamentonun güçlendirilmesi, hem bütçe sürecinde hem de yasama sürecinde çok büyük bir ihtiyaçtır. Bu aynı zamanda bütçe hakkının da ortaya getirdiği bir şeydir.
(Mikrofon otomatik cihaz tarafından kapatıldı)
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (Devamla) - Bahsettiğim bu nedenlerle 2025 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Bütçe Kanunu Teklifi'ne ve 2023 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Kesin Hesap Kanunu Teklifi'ne Cumhuriyet Halk Partisi olarak...
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Bir dakika daha açın Başkanım.
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Bir dakika...
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN (İstanbul) - Bir dakika... Bir dakika...
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Bir daha, baştan alalım.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Bir dakika, selamlama için...
BAŞKAN - Buyurun.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (Devamla) - Sağ olun, teşekkür ederim. Daha anlaşılsın dediniz herhâlde Sayın Başkan.
Evet, bu, yukarıda bahsettiğim nedenlerle, 2025 Yılı Merkezi Yönetim Bütçe Kanunu Teklifi ile 2023 Yılı Kesin Hesap Kanunu Teklifi'ne Cumhuriyet Halk
Partisi olarak "ret" oyu vereceğimizi belirtiyor, yüce heyeti saygıyla selamlıyorum. (CHP sıralarından alkışlar)
records.englishTranslation:
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (İzmir) ON BEHALF OF CHP GROUP - Thank you, Mr. President.
Mr. President, dear members of parliament; I took the floor to express the views of the Republican People's Party on the 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal and the 2023 Final Account Law Proposal. On this occasion, I respectfully greet the General Assembly and our citizens watching us on their screens.
Now, of course, we are discussing the 2025 budget, as well as the final account. These are important texts because, after all, how does a society currently exist?
These are the texts that draw a framework and perspective on this issue, where it is in an environment where it will go in the coming period, what will happen, what policies will be implemented, what measures will be taken, where the country's resources will be transferred, but the interesting thing is this: While we are talking about this budget, we actually see that the problems that Türkiye exists and is experiencing are getting worse; Türkiye is in a very serious crisis in all dimensions, political, economic, social and administrative.
Dear MPs, Türkiye is rapidly shifting from democracy to an authoritarian regime, and this shift towards authoritarianism has accelerated with the 2018 Presidential government system adopted after the 2017 referendum. The separation of powers between the legislature, executive and judiciary, which are the basic organs of the state, has been abolished legally and de facto. In every system - not only parliamentary, parliamentary presidential, semi-presidential, in all systems - the indispensable condition is the balance and control mechanism, but unfortunately, there is no balance and control mechanism in this system. (Applause from CHP benches) The executive has established serious domination over both the legislature and the judiciary.
This system is also a system that is extremely arbitrary. Look, the state structure has changed from top to bottom in this new system; Institutions were closed, merged and restructured, and as if this was not enough, a parallel bureaucratic structure was created within the Presidency. Boards, offices, presidencies and their relationship with classical ministries are extremely problematic,
that relationship is an undefined relationship. Just sitting with Presidential decrees and saying "We established these, these will do this task." Saying this is not enough, friends. Bureaucracy is an experience, bureaucracy is an experience, bureaucracy is a memory, a habit of working together. When you don't do these, that's when you say "We came, we ruled." You say, but you govern Türkiye as it is today. Although it is clear in the Constitution that fundamental rights and freedoms cannot be regulated by Presidential decrees, Presidential decrees are persistently issued on this subject.
Look, dear friends, Presidential decrees that were overturned by the Constitutional Court are coming to the commissions and the Planning and Budget Commission like a rainstorm. Could such an unserious job be possible?

We will discuss an omnibus bill here next week; Article 14 is about the issues regulated by the Presidential decree being overturned and annulled by the Constitutional Court and brought before us to be made into law, but we already know this: Similar regulations were made again, but there were unconstitutionality while the law was being made, and then we went to the Constitutional Court again. Why don't you comply with the Constitution? You have made it a habit to disobey the Constitution. Why are you doing this? Speaking before, on the first day of the budget, "A Constitution that does not enforce itself." The spokesperson of the Justice and Development Party Group said; Friends, is there a Constitution that does not enforce itself? You will abide by the Constitution, the Constitution is a social contract. As a result, "I can comply with where I want, but I can't comply with where I don't want to." If you say, this is not the case. (Applause from CHP benches) This is the Republic of Türkiye; This country has traditions, this is not a banana republic, "I do what I want, I don't do what I don't want." Impossible.
Extraordinary government procedures were normalized during this period of power. Look, there is no state of emergency in the country today, but the rules of state of emergency are valid. of this
The most striking example is the appointment of trustees to local governments. Based on an amended article of law enacted during the state of emergency, when mayors are said to be "connected with terrorism" in any way, trustees are appointed instead of an election from within the municipal council. Such a thing does not happen; This is the usurpation of the national will. National will is not only in the presence of the central government, but also in the presence of local governments. It is necessary to comply with this.
The central government-local government relationship is extremely problematic. You have been ruling the government for twenty-two years, and in the 2019 elections, some municipalities passed to us, and most recently in the 2024 elections, we won both the metropolitan and provincial and district municipalities, and suddenly this appeared before us: Practices such as cutting off the shares of municipalities and municipal companies from the general budget for the collection of overdue tax and insurance premium debts, as well as seizing the accounts of companies, came to the fore. Why weren't you doing this before? You've been in power for twenty-two years, didn't they have any debts? (Applause from CHP benches) Why didn't you take any steps on this issue and now you are doing it? "We lost the municipalities." You do this, right? It doesn't work like that, friends; The central government and local government are a whole, and the central government already has tutelage authority over local governments and is subject to all kinds of control. In other words, "We come, we do what we want." Look, this is arbitrariness; This is the inherent arbitrariness of the presidential government system, it is a one-man regime and an arbitrary regime.
Judiciary, legislature. Let me talk about legislation first. The position of the Parliament has been weakened in this system and the Parliament has become dysfunctional. One of the most basic manifestations of this is that the state organization is regulated by Presidential decrees.
State organization and bureaucracy are not regulated by Presidential decrees. Why is there a legislature, why is there a Parliament? Why did people go to elections? Why did they elect us to this country, to the Parliament, to represent themselves?

You do these by presidential decree. Friends, the parliament does not even have the opportunity to reject the budget. Budgets are important all over the world within the scope of the right to budget, and rejection of the budget - there has been an example in Türkiye in the past - also means the fall of the government. But here, when the budget is rejected, for some reason, the revaluation rate increases, that is, as much as inflation. Look, this is usurpation of budget rights.
Omnibus laws, no specialization has been achieved. Omnibus laws, omnibus laws are coming to the Planning and Budget Commission; matters that are not of our Commission's interest. What are other commissions doing? Look, there is specialization in the establishment of the Parliament and there are relevant commissions, and MPs who are interested in that subject are elected to the relevant commission. But not employing them and having them all come together through this omnibus law system and then discuss the basic law in the Parliament encourages the legislature to make extremely unqualified and low-quality laws. (Cries of "Bravo" from CHP benches, applause)
And even more interestingly, the President is also the Chairman of a political party. There is an extremely strict separation of powers between the legislature and the executive, especially in countries where the presidential system is implemented. So, when we look at this whole system here, it is the domination of the executive over the legislature and the dysfunction of the legislature.
It is a source of shame for the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye that elected MP Can Atalay did not take the oath and begin his duty in the Parliament despite the Constitutional Court's decision and was subsequently stripped of his membership. (Applause from CHP benches)
Members of the higher judicial bodies are elected by the President or the party of which the President is the Chairman and which has a majority in the Parliament.
not true. Dear MPs, the conflict between judicial bodies has reached serious levels. It has happened in the past, we know that the courts do not comply with the decisions of the Constitutional Court many times, but most recently, the problems and conflict between the Constitutional Court and the Supreme Court are extremely serious and when we look at the functioning of the judiciary, it creates serious problems. Of course, this shows that the decisions taken by the judiciary are discussed in public, dear MPs. Look, let's know this: As the judiciary loses its legitimacy in the conscience of the society, the mentality of seeking the solution in mafia relations and criminal organizations becomes widespread. (Applause from CHP ranks) The complete establishment of the judiciary, the rule of law and judicial independence, and the principle of equal legal security for all citizens are indispensable for a democracy. Fundamental rights and freedoms mean that all citizens have the freedom of thought and expression, they can express their thoughts freely, but we know that there are attempts to impose restrictions on the freedom of citizens to express their thoughts through practices such as the censorship law called the "disinformation law" and influence agency. Democracy is also the right to criticism. Look, let's not think of democracy as consisting only of the ballot box, which is visited every four or five years.

This is a necessary condition for democracy, it is a formal condition, it is indispensable, but what is at least as important is the enrichment of democracy in terms of essence and content. This is the development of fundamental rights and freedoms, the right to meetings and demonstrations, and the right to a fair trial. When all these things happen, democracy is established in that country. Of course, political
These are the problems in the field. I wonder what the field of economics is like, I would like to look at it. I will look at it from the perspective of accumulation, growth and distribution. There is an economic model that has lost its development perspective. I repeat, it is a model that has lost its development perspective. The neoliberal economic model continues with all its institutions and rules, and during the twenty-two-year AKP period, the structural problems of the economy have not been solved, on the contrary, they have become aggravated, and the most important of this is an economy that is based on external resources and has a current account deficit due to foreign resources. Now, during the budget presentation, both the Vice President and the Minister of Treasury and Finance said, "We are reducing the current account deficit." They say, but dear friends, of course energy has an impact on the current account deficit as imports, there is also gold imports, but essentially Türkiye's problem is that Türkiye's production and export structure is dependent on imports of intermediate goods. Unless you change this, you cannot solve the current account deficit problem in Türkiye. You also know that you cannot solve it. Although you have mentioned this in every plan, every medium-term program, every annual program and budget that we have prepared for twenty-two years, there is no way forward in this area, but this year the current account deficit seems to have improved a little due to the slowdown of the economy and these reasons, but the current account deficit cannot be corrected like this. The quality of growth is weak, low investments, especially there is a serious decrease in public investments, and the investments made by the private sector do not replace, replace or compensate for these investments. However, there is a complementarity relationship between public investments and private investments in economics. Public investments encourage private investments, but in Türkiye, the public, especially with this public-private cooperation model, has withdrawn from the field of serious investment in physical and social infrastructure, especially in transportation, energy and hospitals, most recently in city hospitals.
He gave these areas to the private sector, but what the private sector should normally do is to increase production and exports in the country. The field of infrastructure investments is the public domain and they go in a relationship of efficiency with each other.
The perspective of industrialization has been lost. "We will produce high value-added goods and services in all documents." Although you say, unfortunately Türkiye is far from this. The expected leap in technology intensity has not been achieved. When we look at the total, the share of the manufacturing industry and high-tech sectors is only 3 percent. Will Türkiye compete with this? Will Türkiye compete with developing countries like us - let alone developed countries? With Indonesia, Malaysia, Argentina and Brazil? In this way, with this model, Türkiye will not go anywhere.
Agriculture has been liquidated. Look, today food security is a strategic sector all over the world and food security is important.
There is impoverishing growth that does not create employment.

You say that unemployment rates are around 8 percent, 9 percent, but when you look at the idle workforce - which TÜİK calculates - it is at least twice that; It is not 9 percent, it is 28 percent, 29 percent. Not 3.5 million people, but 9-10 million people are unemployed in Türkiye. He is unemployed because he wants to work and is at an age where he can work, but does not participate in the workforce for some reason. Inflation is at high levels and the relative price structure is deteriorating. Dear MPs, Turkey has been implementing an inflation targeting regime since 2006, and at first the target was as follows: An inflation rate of 3 percent, 4 percent, 5 percent, but Turkey could never achieve these. Not only can it not be achieved, but we have been faced with extremely high inflation rates in recent years, as I will explain in a moment. Income and wealth distribution is unequal and unfair. Poverty is both widespread and deepening, and public services are failing to meet social needs.
It's too far. It is inadequate both in quantity and quality. Working life is one of the areas where problems are greatest. Not only are labor force participation rates and employment rates low, informality is still very high, with almost 1 in four working unregistered. Unionization rates are very low, occupational health and safety is inadequate. Look, work accidents, which are occupational murders, we experience them in almost all areas today, from the mining industry to the construction industry.
There are strikes, which are the most basic bargaining power of the working class, and you are constantly postponing the strikes. There will be strikes; The right to strike within that society, the right to strike under collective agreement, is ultimately the most fundamental right of the workers. How else will you reach a compromise? You do not convene the Economic and Social Council, you have not convened it since February 2009. I submitted questions, still nothing. Here, it is mentioned with some expressions such as "to be collected". Is it possible to do such an unserious thing? Is this your pleasure? (Applause from the CHP ranks) You comply with the rules in the Constitution when it suits you, you do not comply when it does not suit you, and as a result of all this, the rates of violence increase; corruption and criminal economy are increasing; Gangs and mafia exist everywhere and there is corruption, corruption and decay in values. Friends, we last experienced the newborn gang incident, our blood ran cold. It's a structure that kills babies because of money, and this has been going on for a long time, and maybe when we look at it, it's just the tip of the iceberg, who knows what else is underneath? And the fact that such a structure is becoming more and more widespread actually shows this: So, the sense of justice, that judgment, that view that the judiciary can solve justice, is disappearing.
Development plan, MTP, budget relationship. Look, a budget is a resource allocation mechanism. Dear MPs, they are political as well as technical texts, in other words, the state confiscates a part of the citizen's income through taxation and then distributes it to certain expenses. There are certain questions here, two basic questions. The answer you give to this question determines the orientation, outlook and ideological position of that government, the political power. One, from whom do you collect taxes? What types of taxes do you collect and from whom?

Second, in which areas do you spend and who benefits from this? The answers to these questions are the government's choice. When we look at it from this perspective, this is a budget that is no different from previous budgets and is against labor and in favor of capital. It is, it shouldn't be like this. Especially in these crisis conditions we are experiencing, the same existing structure continues, even though the opposite should happen. Development plan, MTP, annual program, budget. Look, these are top policy documents and there is harmony and consistency between these top policy documents everywhere in the world, but this is not the case in Türkiye. The development plan has nothing to do with the medium-term program. It is not even possible to compare the figures of 2028, the last year of the development plan, and 2027, the last year of the medium-term program. So, what happened? Did we throw it away? Just a year has passed and we have thrown away the development plan. So what happened then? Look, Türkiye has a serious planning experience that started in the 1960s. You closed the State Planning Organization, but planning continues in some way, but let's do this planning well. Planning means a road map, a vision. Where are we today, where will we go tomorrow? What vehicles will we use, what expenses will we make? It is a framework related to this, and in this sense, these documents no longer provide any guidance to the decision makers, households and companies in the economy.
The main perspective of the MTP and the budget is disinflation, dear friends; disinflation, controlling and reducing inflation. Well, but won't we ask the following question: Why is Türkiye in a disinflation process now? Because you exploded inflation, because in September 2021, by applying the President's "interest is the cause, inflation is the result" thesis, the Central Bank - which is the duty given to it by law to ensure price stability, that is, to protect the value of the Turkish lira - inflation exploded, the dollar exchange rate went down, the balances of the entire economy were disrupted; Now, you are trying to reduce the inflation you increased. And how do you do this? It is very clear there too - both the new Minister of Treasury and Finance and the Central Bank Governor at that time, who took office after the 2023 elections, said so - a return to rational policies, a transition from irrational policies to rational policies. You are the ones implementing good but irrational policies. Why did you implement it? Why did you put Türkiye into such a wrong practice in September 2021, when the Central Bank policy rate was 19 percent, inflation was 19.25 percent and the dollar exchange rate was 8 lira 30 kuruş, and as a result you exploded inflation? And what are you trying to do now? Reducing total demand, suppressing foreign exchange and eliminating cost inflation through tight monetary, tight fiscal and tight incomes policies, as well as capital inflows from abroad. Good but look, you couldn't break inflation expectations, inflation expectations are very high and I see it often, especially there is an approach that finds the cause of inflation in wage increases; This is not true.

Of course, wages are a cost element, we do not deny that, but other than that, the inflation that Türkiye has been experiencing recently is profit-driven inflation.
There are high profits. Look, this is also discussed in the literature, there are articles on this. Enter the ISO 500 companies and look at their profitability rates. There is a structure pushed by profits. The Central Bank already shyly expresses this in its press releases. Do you know what he says? "Pricing behavior in the economy has deteriorated." Well, this is the report and on the one hand you are doing this, you cannot break expectations, you cannot change the incomplete, competitive structure of the markets and their monopolistic and oligopolistic structure. Today, companies in certain sectors in Türkiye dominate the market and dictate the price as they wish. Unless you change this, you cannot permanently reduce inflation to low single-digit levels. There is no structural reform, no tax reform, no spending reform, even savings measures are for show. We see this from the spending made by the President. You cannot reduce inflation this way. The Minister of Treasury and Finance is starting the milestone with himself and has painted a rosy picture in the economy. Mr. Vice President also does it. "We reduced CDS rates." You boast, "We increased the reserves." You say that credit rating agencies increased our country's rating by two notches. Good, but you are the one who has been ruling Türkiye for twenty-two years, you are the one who increased the CDS rates to 900, you are the one who reduced the reserves to negative levels. You are the one who lowered the credit rating agencies' rating from "investment grade" to "extremely speculative" level. (Applause from CHP benches) So you don't make any self-criticism, at all! In other words, "These problems happened, we made a mistake, let's fix it." We cannot see such an approach in any policy document or presentation. For God's sake, one makes self-criticism, he says. All the problems lie in external dynamics, not external forces. All of this stems from the wrong policies you implement internally. Know this and come out, say it, admit it, "We did wrong, we will fix it." You say but you don't and Türkiye 2021 September-
The period until May 2023 and now "inflation will decrease to single digits by the end of 2026." It was said; He lost a period of approximately five to five and a half years. These are Türkiye's lost years. While Türkiye can seriously grow its economy and increase its investments here; While it could have access to resources that could solve many problems in education and health, it has wasted its resources with these wrong policies. The social cost of the fight against inflation has been placed on the workers, civil servants, retirees, farmers, tradesmen and artisans living in this country, and now it seems that it will continue to be burdened during the upcoming disinflation process. If you remember, we were going to have single-digit inflation at the end of 2026, which was 9.7 percent - just like a single digit; It was only 10 percent below 0.3 points - but now, just now, when the MTP was in full bloom, the Central Bank made a change and brought it down to 12 percent. So, it seems that single-digit inflation is postponed until 2027, but unfortunately, the people who bear the burden of this policy will continue to bear this burden.

The 2025 budget, I would like to touch on it briefly. When we look at the budget deficit, it is decreasing. Year-end inflation was 17.5 percent in the MTP, the Central Bank increased it to 21 percent, but of course, these are year-end, we need to look at the average inflation when looking at this. In other words, the gross domestic product deflator is 33.9 percent - which will also rise, it will most likely exceed 40 percent - the increase in expenditures is 31 percent, and the increase in tax revenues is 46.5 percent. You cut spending and increase taxes. Here, what is this budget? It is very clear here who he serves. (Applause from the CHP ranks) There is interest expenditure as much as the budget deficit, 1 trillion 950 billion liras of interest expenditure; when you take it off it goes even lower
It has fallen, and very interestingly, investment expenditures in the budget - I mean nominally, in absolute numbers - are only increasing by 8 per thousand. See what a negative level it is when we adjust it for inflation in real terms. Even capital transfers are nominal; It has decreased by 56 percent in absolute terms, a very serious decrease in real terms. In other words, non-interest expenditures also show the capacity of the economy and the public to create and produce services. The capacity to produce services is decreasing. The increase in tax revenues is very high, the tax burden is increasing. Here, both the Vice President and the Minister of Treasury and Finance have always said that Türkiye's tax burden is not very high among OECD countries, but look, it has been increasing over the years, there is an increase of 2.5 points in the last three years; Let's say it once.
Two: Türkiye's tax burden may not be very high in OECD countries, but countries with high tax burdens also have high public expenditures, they are at much higher levels; Scandinavian countries and other countries. In this sense, it is not possible to look at the issue only in terms of tax burden, you will also look at others, you will look at public expenditures, and when we look at the tax burden in terms of indirect and direct tax burden, the burden of indirect taxes, that is, taxes such as SCT and VAT collected on goods and services, is extremely high. Direct taxes - Turkey is at this point because it has not made a serious tax reform. The distorted structure of 65 percent to 35 percent based on indirect taxes continues.
Tax expenditures. For as long as I can remember, since the bureaucracy, since my bureaucratic years at the State Planning Organization, almost every plan and program has stated, "Tax expenditures, that is, taxes waived through exceptions, exemptions and deductions, will be reviewed in terms of effectiveness and will be narrowed down." Although it is written, it has not been narrowed down but has increased.
Look, the budget deficit for 2025 is approximately
It is 2 trillion, the tax given up is 3 trillion. Giving up 3 trillion liras of tax is extremely problematic. The amount of money to be spent on the PPP model and public-private partnership model in the next three years is 689 billion lira. The burden of the exchange rate protected deposit system; This is the budget deficit, you will put this in the budget deficit; This is the budget deficit. (Applause from CHP ranks) It will be 830 billion liras in 2023, the Central Bank did not announce it this year either, but when we look at the other item in the assets of the Central Bank's balance sheet, it is understood that it exceeds 350 billion liras.
Distribution relations have been disrupted, dear friends.

According to income groups, the 5 percent with the lowest income receives 1 percent of the income, while the richest 5 percent receives 23.9 percent, a full 24 times more, and again, according to a study conducted by some international research institutions and banks, the richest 1 percent in Türkiye receives 40 percent of the wealth in the country, and the remaining 60 percent is shared by 99 percent. Is there such justice? How can we ensure peace and prosperity in this country, how can you ensure it? And even more interestingly, there is no perspective on this in the upcoming development plan and medium-term program. The issue of income distribution is like a thing. In fact, it is understood from the statements of the Minister of Treasury and Finance, he says: "First, we will reduce inflation to single digits, then the situation of workers, civil servants and retirees will improve. Cover it up and die. Is there such a thing, friends? What Türkiye needs is a development strategy, to return to development again. In the 101st year of our Republic, Türkiye should act on this basis.
1) High growth rates must be ensured; Türkiye should be able to achieve growth rates of 6 percent, 7 percent and has achieved it in the past.
2) A people-oriented development; balanced, qualified, inclusive. Not just growth, but our people's
is to increase living standards, welfare and quality of life. Housing, education, health, social security; In all these areas, a social state needs to be fully reconstructed and exist.
3) Development should include future generations, sustainability is important. One of the pillars of this is protecting the environment and not destroying nature, increasing our capacity to produce technology, ending privatization policies, and transferring public resources to pro-government capital groups through racketeering auctions. (Applause from CHP benches) This must be stopped and policies that will put future generations in debt must be stopped. The exchange rate protected deposit system is a serious burden today - although we will see when it will be resolved. Public-private cooperation is a structure that will put future generations, our children, grandchildren, and the unborn in debt, and for this, the planning-market relationship must be re-established, and planning and the market must allocate resources together. Of course, all of these, as I said at the beginning, need to be done by improving fundamental rights and freedoms within democracy and the rule of law.
Finally, I would like to focus on the right to budget. Look, I would like to say a few technical things about the budget process.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN - Mr. Rahmi, here you go.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (Continuing) - Thank you, Mr. President.
The budget process is far from effective, there is a need for a qualified and comprehensive discussion of the budget in the Turkish Grand National Assembly, and in this sense, the Turkish Grand National Assembly needs to be specialized in this regard. First of all, the budget systematics should be improved, in the past there were extra-budgetary funds, revolving funds - revolving funds continue - special account, then agencies were established, foundations were established, look, what needs to be is a transparent budget, a single treasury, the principle of unity of the budget and accountability. There is Türkiye Wealth Fund, it is exchange rate protected
There is a deposit system. All of these need to be taken as a whole.

2) Development plans are realized and put forward as a decision of the Turkish Grand National Assembly. We are here. In other words, it is a decision of the Turkish Grand National Assembly, not a legalization process. Then, there is a need to present the implementation results of the development plan to the Turkish Grand National Assembly annually. We look and see what has been done and what has not been done. Establishing a final account commission is of great importance.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
CHAIRMAN - Here you go.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (Continuing) - Thank you.
A final accounting commission should be established, a permanent commission will work together with the Planning and Budget Commission, its chairman will be from the main opposition party and the budget implementation results will be evaluated in every sense; will seriously examine it in terms of procedure and merits. Türkiye needs this. (Applause from CHP benches) Türkiye needs to use its resources well. We have to do this for Türkiye, for our ancestors, for our children and grandchildren.
The scope and effectiveness of the Court of Accounts audit should be increased. Look, Article 45 of the Court of Accounts Law is very interesting; It authorizes the Grand National Assembly of Türkiye and the Court of Accounts to audit any institution it requests to audit, as it wishes.
Mr. President, this is a very important issue; I don't know if it is used or not, but I will bring it to the agenda with a parliamentary question regarding this issue. In other words, in this regard, strengthening the Turkish Grand National Assembly, the legislature and the Parliament is a great need both in the budget process and in the legislative process. This is also something that the right to budget brings about.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (Continue) - For the reasons I mentioned, we, as the Republican People's Party, support the 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal and the 2023 Central Government Final Account Law Proposal.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - Open for one more minute, President.
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Wait a minute.
GÖKHAN GÜNAYDIN ​​(Istanbul) - Wait a minute. One minute.
SİBEL SUİÇMEZ (Trabzon) - Let's start from the beginning again.
CAVİT ARI (Antalya) - One minute, for greeting.
CHAIRMAN - Here you go.
RAHMİ AŞKIN TÜRELİ (Continuing) - Thank you, thank you. I guess you said it would be more understandable, Mr. President.
Yes, for the reasons I mentioned above, the 2025 Central Government Budget Law Proposal and the 2023 Final Account Law Proposal were approved by the Republican People's Party.
I state that, as our party, we will vote "no" and I respectfully salute the noble delegation. (Applause from CHP benches)
